message:
fix: use configured topology policy in all project hash call sites

Three bugs found during fresh-eyes review of the path_topology feature:

1. build_sync_closure_plan() computed dependency root hashes via
   compute_project_hash() which hardcodes PathTopologyPolicy::default(),
   ignoring the user-configured topology_policy already in scope. On
   macOS with custom roots this would produce different hashes for the
   same dependency, causing unnecessary cache invalidation.

2. execute_remote_compilation() used compute_project_hash() for custom
   CARGO_TARGET_DIR pipelines, same default-policy bug.

3. PathTopologyConfig::to_policy() passed raw strings through without
   tilde expansion or empty-string filtering. A user writing
   canonical_root = "~/Projects" (the exact macOS use case) would get
   PathBuf("~/Projects") which is not absolute and fails normalization.
   Empty strings bypassed the default fallback entirely.

Fixes: filter empty strings to fall back to defaults, expand ~ via
shellexpand::tilde(), and replace both compute_project_hash() call
sites with the policy-aware variant. The convenience wrappers are now
#[cfg(test)] only since production code should always use the
explicit-policy version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
